_provenance.note: Second real paper end-to-end (Epic V): Nesterov's accelerated gradient method (Nesterov 1983, 'A method for solving the convex programming problem with convergence rate O(1/k^2)') lifted from prose into a grounded dgroup by an in-session Claude Code sub-agent (no API call), in the style of Epic K (arXiv 2006.06138). Decomposed against retrieval/library/nesterov_priors.json: the residual after covering should be the OPTIMAL_RATE leaf (and the MOMENTUM -> ACCELERATED_CONVERGENCE cause) -- the paper's genuine contribution over plain gradient descent. The structural property MOMENTUM plays the same PC/2 role discovered across the four literatures, so the paper also joins the cross-domain analogy web.

target.name: nesterov_acceleration
target.text: On a smooth convex function, Nesterov's accelerated gradient method uses a momentum extrapolation step to attain the optimal convergence rate O(1/k^2), quadratically faster than the lower bound achieved by plain gradient descent.
